factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in truckload services, leading to higher costs and tighter carrier capacity.

evidence.text: The average routing guide depth of tender in the third quarter of 2020 was 1.6, representing that on average, the first or second carrier in a shipper's routing guide was executing the shipment in most cases. This routing guide penetration compares to 1.2 in both the first and second quarters of 2020 and is reflective of the increasing consumer demand and production and the tightening carrier capacity evident in the third quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to the ongoing uncertainty of the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on future results.

evidence.text: The extent to which the COVID-19 pandemic impacts our financial results and operations for the remainder of 2020 and going forward will depend on future developments which are highly uncertain and cannot be predicted.
evidence.subsection: SIGNIFICANT DEVELOPMENTS

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on gaining market share through strategic acquisitions and expanding its global logistics capabilities.

evidence.text: On March 2, 2020, we acquired all of the outstanding shares of Prime Distribution Services (''Prime Distribution''), a leading provider of retail consolidation services in North America for $222.7 million in cash. This acquisition adds scale and value-added warehouse capabilities to our retail consolidation platform, adding to our global suite of services.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

factors.summary: A specific catalyst is the acquisition of Prime Distribution, which has added scale and capabilities to the company's logistics platform.

evidence.text: On March 2, 2020, we acquired all of the outstanding shares of Prime Distribution Services (''Prime Distribution''), a leading provider of retail consolidation services in North America for $222.7 million in cash. This acquisition adds scale and value-added warehouse capabilities to our retail consolidation platform, adding to our global suite of services.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share, particularly in higher-margin segments like airfreight and ocean services, despite industry challenges.

evidence.text: Net revenues decreased 7.0 percent to $589.3 million, primarily driven by rising costs and lower margin in truckload services, partially offset by contributions from the acquisition of Prime Distribution and higher pricing in most of our service lines.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying due to capacity shortages and pricing pressures in the logistics industry.

evidence.text: The global forwarding market also showed signs of improvement during the third quarter of 2020 after multiple quarters of reduced demand and production resulting in decreased volumes due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Many companies began to replenish low inventory levels amidst continued market uncertainty from the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include its global network and ability to integrate acquired companies effectively.

evidence.text: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. (''C.H. Robinson,'' ''the company,'' ''we,'' ''us,'' or ''our'') is one of the largest third party logistics companies in the world. As a third party logistics provider, we enter into contractual relationships with a wide variety of transportation companies and utilize those relationships to efficiently and cost-effectively arrange the transport of our customers’ freight.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, but there is still risk from dependence on certain industries like retail and food and beverage.

evidence.text: We provide freight transportation services and logistics solutions to companies of a wide variety of industries. We operate through a network of offices in North America, Europe, Asia, Oceania, and South America. We have developed global transportation and distribution networks to provide transportation and supply chain services worldwide.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: The acquisition of Prime Distribution is a key new initiative, providing enhanced warehouse capabilities and contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: On March 2, 2020, we acquired all of the outstanding shares of Prime Distribution Services (''Prime Distribution''), a leading provider of retail consolidation services in North America for $222.7 million in cash. This acquisition adds scale and value-added warehouse capabilities to our retail consolidation platform, adding to our global suite of services.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

factors.summary: Acquisition activity has contributed to revenue growth, particularly through the acquisition of Prime Distribution.

evidence.text: The acquisition of Prime Distribution contributed 4.0 percentage points to LTL net revenue growth in the third quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Economic conditions are challenging due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, creating headwinds for the company.

evidence.text: The company is monitoring the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, which has already caused a significant disruption to global financial markets and supply chains and has resulted in increased travel restrictions and extended shutdown of certain businesses across the globe. We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors Overview

factors.summary: Interest rate impacts have been favorable, with lower rates reducing interest expenses.

evidence.text: Interest expense decreased driven by a lower average debt balance and lower interest rates in the nine months ended September 30, 2020 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have had a favorable impact on financial results.

evidence.text: Interest and other expense. Interest and other expense primarily consisted of interest expense of $36.6 million partially offset by a $2.2 million favorable impact of foreign currency revaluation and realized foreign currency gains and losses in the nine months ended September 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different markets, with some experiencing growth and others facing declines, though there are signs of accelerating demand in certain key industries.

evidence.text: The North American surface transportation market experienced increasing sequential demand during the third quarter of 2020 despite many small to mid-sized companies and certain industries continuing to experience demand below historical levels due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company's pricing power has shown mixed results across different service segments. While there has been success in raising prices in ocean and airfreight services, driven by increased demand and capacity constraints, other segments like truckload have faced margin compression, indicating varying effectiveness of pricing strategies.

evidence.text: Total revenues increased 9.6 percent to $4.2 billion, driven primarily by higher pricing in most service lines, most notably in ocean and airfreight services.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides. Total purchased transportation and related services increased driven by the high cost for airfreight and ocean capacity in the marketplace due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic in addition to increased volume in truckload and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth, with a 7.1% decrease in net income compared to the prior year. This deceleration is attributed to market conditions and pricing challenges, particularly in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on transportation costs and capacity.

evidence.subsection: CONSOLIDATED RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides. Total purchased transportation and related services increased driven by the high cost for airfreight and ocean capacity in the marketplace due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic in addition to increased volume in truckload and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments such as airfreight and ocean services, which is contributing positively to net revenues. However, this shift is partially offset by challenges in truckload services, including margin compression and rising costs.

evidence.text: Net revenues decreased 7.0 percent to $589.3 million, primarily driven by rising costs and lower margin in truckload services, partially offset by contributions from the acquisition of Prime Distribution and higher pricing in most of our service lines.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Net revenues. Our transportation net revenue margin decreased driven by margin compression in truckload services due to the tight carrier capacity in the marketplace and the significant transportation cost volatility resulting from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic relative to our contractual customer pricing. We have continued to meet our customer commitments despite moderating declines or, in some instances, increases for the cost of capacity, which has resulted in margin compression. Partially offsetting these declines was an increase in airfreight pricing resulting in margin expansion as we were able to leverage our contractual airfreight capacity despite significant shortages in the airfreight market.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Volumes are showing mixed trends, with growth in truckload and LTL services but declines in airfreight.

evidence.text: Our NAST truckload and LTL volumes increased 0.5 percent and 13.5 percent, respectively, which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides. The airfreight market has been significantly impacted by reduced cargo capacity due to fewer commercial flights, an increase in charter flights, and larger than normal shipment sizes which has created an environment with unusually high pricing.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with retail and food and beverage sectors showing growth, while truckload and LTL services also experience growth. However, the food service industry faces challenges due to the pandemic, and small to mid-sized companies are recovering more slowly.

evidence.text: The improving demand during the third quarter of 2020 was most pronounced with large companies and from the retail and food and beverage industries.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides. The food service industry has been significantly impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, leading to margin compression. This increase is driven by higher transportation costs, particularly in airfreight and ocean services, as well as truckload and LTL services.

evidence.text: Truckload transportation costs, excluding fuel prices, increased at a faster rate resulting in margin compression.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services increased driven by the high cost for airfreight and ocean capacity in the marketplace due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic in addition to increased volume in truckload and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Labor costs have been managed effectively through various measures, leading to a 5.5% decrease in personnel expenses. The reduction is attributed to temporary salary cuts, furloughs, and decreased work hours, with the average headcount also declining by 5.6%. Additionally, short-term cost reductions and a 2.6% drop in average headcount contributed to the decline in labor costs.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased 5.5 percent to $302.9 million, driven primarily by cost reductions. Average headcount decreased 5.6 percent, despite the Prime acquisition contributing approximately two percentage points of growth.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to declines in variable compensation and short-term cost reductions, including furloughs and reduced work hours. Average headcount decreased 2.6 percent.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure, primarily due to rising costs and challenges in maintaining pricing, which have led to compression in both gross and operating margins. The transportation sector has been particularly affected, with net revenue margins decreasing due to tight carrier capacity and transportation cost volatility from the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to rising costs and lower net revenue margins.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Operating margin of 28.6 percent decreased 310 basis points.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

evidence.text: Our transportation net revenue margin decreased driven by margin compression in truckload services due to the tight carrier capacity in the marketplace and the significant transportation cost volatility resulting from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic relative to our contractual customer pricing.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The supply chain health is negatively impacted by disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to challenges in maintaining normal lead times, increased transportation costs, and strained infrastructure.

evidence.text: We have taken a variety of measures to ensure the availability, continuity, and security of our critical infrastructure, ensure the health and safety of our employees around the globe, and provide service and supply chain continuity to our customers and contracted carriers in order to deliver critical and essential goods and services.
evidence.subsection: SIGNIFICANT DEVELOPMENTS

evidence.text: The airfreight market has been significantly impacted by reduced cargo capacity due to fewer commercial flights, an increase in charter flights, and larger than normal shipment sizes which has created an environment with unusually high pricing.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs have been implemented across the organization, including temporary salary reductions, furloughs, elimination of non-essential travel, and reduced work hours, resulting in measurable savings. The company has also taken steps to further reduce costs by suspending retirement plan matches and accelerating the use of paid time off.

evidence.text: We have taken steps across the organization to reduce costs, including the elimination of all non-essential travel, temporary salary reductions for company executive officers, temporary reductions in cash retainers for board members, temporary suspension of the company match to retirement plans for U.S. and Canadian employees, accelerating the use of paid time off, and furloughing approximately seven percent of our U.S and Canadian employees in the second quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: SIGNIFICANT DEVELOPMENTS

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to declines in variable compensation and short-term cost reductions, including furloughs and reduced work hours. Average headcount decreased 2.6 percent.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019

factors.summary: The near-term risks are predominantly negative, with significant disruptions from the COVID-19 pandemic impacting demand and supply chains, leading to reduced production, decreased freight volumes, and increased costs from capacity shortages and higher carrier charges.

evidence.text: Many businesses continue to experience reduced production and output which could result in a decrease in freight volumes across a number of industries which may reduce our contractual and spot-market opportunities.
evidence.subsection: SIGNIFICANT DEVELOPMENTS

evidence.text: In addition, a significant number of our contracted carriers may reduce their capacity or charge higher prices in light of the volatile market conditions which may reduce our net revenue margins as we honor our contractual freight rates.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors Overview

evidence.text: The company is monitoring the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, which has already caused a significant disruption to global financial markets and supply chains and has resulted in increased travel restrictions and extended shutdown of certain businesses across the globe.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors Overview

evidence.text: We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbiate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors Overview

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to changes in demand due to the pandemic, fuel prices, and global economic conditions.

evidence.text: Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, including uncertain consumer demand; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services; changes due to catastrophic events including pandemics such as COVID-19; competition and growth rates within the third party logistics industry; freight levels and increasing costs and availability of truck capacity or alternative means of transporting freight; changes in relationships with existing contracted truck, rail, ocean, and air carriers; changes in our customer base due to possible consolidation among our customers; our ability to successfully integrate the operations of acquired companies with our historic operations; risks associated with litigation, including contingent auto liability and insurance coverage; risks associated with operations outside of the United States; risks associated with the potential impact of changes in government regulations; risks associated with the produce industry, including food safety and contamination issues; fuel price increases or decreases, or fuel shortages; cyber-security related risks; the impact of war on the economy; changes to our capital structure; risks related to the elimination of LIBOR; and other risks and uncertainties detailed in our Annual and Quarterly Reports.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

evidence.text: The effective income tax rate for the nine months ended September 30, 2020 was lower than the statutory federal income tax rate primarily due to the tax impact of share-based payment awards, including the tax benefit from the delivery of a one-time deferred stock award that was granted to the company's prior Chief Executive Officer in 2000, which reduced the rate by 4.1 percentage points in addition to foreign tax impacts which reduced the rate by 3.1 percentage points.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020 compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2019
